description: A miniature virtual city surrounds a central pool that acts as a magical reflective portal. The user manipulates custom shaders controlling water caustics, city emission glow, day-night cycle, and atmospheric fog — all governed by an energy conservation system where boosting one effect drains others. Undo/redo lets the user step through shader states.
title: City Pool Shader Energy Balancer
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads both pool.glb and VirtualCity.glb via GLTFLoader. Place the pool model at the scene center (y=0). Scale and position VirtualCity around the pool so buildings encircle it like a courtyard plaza. Use OrbitControls for camera interaction, starting at a 45-degree elevated angle looking down at the pool. Add ambient light (low intensity) and a directional light representing the sun.

Implement four custom ShaderMaterial effects, each controlled by a slider (0-100): (1) Water Caustics — apply a custom fragment shader to the pool water surface using animated Voronoi-based caustic patterns with a time uniform; (2) City Glow — apply an emissive post-process or ShaderMaterial override on city buildings using a bloom-like additive color in the fragment shader; (3) Day-Night Cycle — a uniform controlling sun directional light intensity and sky background color interpolation from bright blue to dark navy; (4) Atmospheric Fog — a custom distance-based fog calculation injected into all materials via onBeforeCompile, with density controlled by the slider.

Implement an ENERGY CONSERVATION system: total energy across all four sliders is capped at 200. When the user increases one slider, the others proportionally decrease to maintain the cap. Display the total energy as a bar. Each slider change is a discrete state. Implement undo (Ctrl+Z) and redo (Ctrl+Y) with a history stack of up to 50 states storing all four slider values. On undo/redo, restore slider positions and update all shader uniforms accordingly.

Add a HUD overlay showing: four labeled sliders with numeric readouts, total energy bar, and undo/redo buttons. Style the HUD with semi-transparent dark panels. Animate caustics using a time uniform updated in the render loop. Ensure all shader uniforms update in real-time as sliders move. The render loop should call requestAnimationFrame continuously. Use sRGB encoding and tone mapping for visual quality.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  causticIntensity: number,         // water caustic shader strength 0-100
  cityGlowIntensity: number,         // city emission glow strength 0-100
  dayNightCycle: number,         // sun intensity and sky color 0-100, 0=night 100=day
  fogDensity: number,         // atmospheric fog density 0-100
  totalEnergyBudget: number,         // sum of all four intensities, must equal 200
  sunLightIntensity: number,         // directional light intensity derived from dayNightCycle
  skyColorHue: number,         // background color hue value 0-360
  poolModelLoaded: boolean,         // true when pool.glb is loaded and positioned
  cityModelLoaded: boolean,         // true when VirtualCity.glb is loaded and positioned
  shadersActive: boolean,         // true when all four custom shaders are applied
  energyBalanced: boolean,         // true when totalEnergyBudget equals 200
  lastSliderChanged: string,         // name of most recently adjusted slider
}
